en: [skGull] No official English guide provided.
Sends a prompt to the Groq LPU API using a specific model (Llama 3.3). This is an asynchronous effect, meaning it won't lag the server while waiting for the AI to respond.
Creates a virtual world border that is only visible and active for specific players. This does not affect the actual global world border.
Forcefully resets the click-hold timer for a player. Useful for 'cancelling' a charge-up action or resetting state after a specific game event.
Returns the current time in milliseconds that a player has been holding down a click (Left or Right).
Checks if a player is currently holding down a specific mouse button. This relies on the ClickManager's tracked state.

Shows or removes a 'fake' world border effect for specific players. When shown, this typically creates a red vignette/warning effect on the player's screen without actually restricting their movement based on the global world border.
Opens a sophisticated paged GUI for a player. Automatically handles pagination, navigation buttons, and custom row sizes.
Sets a specific slot in a player's currently open skGull PagedMenu to a given item. This updates both the internal menu state and the live inventory.
Spawns ENTITY_EFFECT particles with precise RGB color control. Compatible with Minecraft 1.21+.
Launches an entity that bounces off walls, floors, and ceilings with configurable energy retention.
Sets the player's sorting priority in the tab list. Lower numbers appear higher up.
Adds an enchantment glint (shiny effect) to an item. Uses the 1.21+ glint override with a legacy fallback (Enchantment + Hide Flags).
[skGull] No official English guide provided.
Checks if an entity is within a cuboid region defined by two locations.
Checks if a player has a clear line of sight to an entity (not blocked by walls).
Enable or disable the AI (awareness) of one or more mobs. Disabled mobs will not move or target players.
Smoothly animates a specific part of an armor stand to a new rotation over a period of ticks.
Indices: 0:head, 1:body, 2:left arm, 3:right arm, 4:left leg, 5:right leg
Causes an entity to emit a specific sound at regular intervals. If a sound is already being emitted, starting a new one will replace it.
Allows or disallows an entity from opening and passing through doors.
This increases the mob's follow range and updates their pathfinding flags.
It also enables a physical door-opening task if the 'allow' syntax is used.
Forces a mob to indefinitely patrol a list of locations. If the mob acquires a target, it will pause the patrol to fight, then resume once the target is lost.
Removes the enchantment glint (shiny effect) from an item, including the 1.21+ glint override.
Removes an existing zone by its name.
Triggers the exact moment an entity enters a player's field of view and is not obstructed by blocks. (Works best with default FOV)
Triggers once an entity enters/exit a set zone.
Calculates and executes a path for an entity to a specific location using the built-in Minecraft pathfinder.
Forces a mob to follow a list of locations sequentially. The pathing will pause if the mob acquires a combat target and resume once the target is cleared.
Performs a 'thick' raycast (sphere-based) from a location to detect entities and blocks, with an optional result storage.
Returns the specific part of an entity (head, body, or feet) that was hit/looked at in a look event.
Creates and registers a new zone using two locations as the corners of a cuboid.
Returns a list of all currently active zones.
Is a continuous state check that triggers repeatedly as long as the player keeps an entity in their sight
